title: aconitum × mariae nothosubsp. mariae
intro: Aconitum × mariae nothosubsp. mariae, also known as Aconitum mariae, is a hybrid plant species within the Aconitum genus, which is a type of monk's hood or wolf's bane plant. The plant contains aconitine, a powerful and potentially toxic alkaloid that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Aconitum mariae include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which have been used to treat conditions such as pain, fever, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Aconitum mariae include tinctures, infusions, and poultices, but due to its toxicity, it should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
